expectations.evidence: Naive Claude might know the general webpack pattern of extending configurations but would not know the specific Conduction convention of using plugins.push() rather than replacing the plugins array. This is a project-specific convention.

Without specific knowledge of the @nextcloud/webpack-vue-config base configuration and which plugins it defines, naive Claude would not know to preserve the existing DefinePlugin. It might overwrite it.

The conditional fs.existsSync check for ../nextcloud-vue/src is Conduction-specific (handles monorepo local dev vs npm package). Naive Claude would not know this pattern or that @conduction/nextcloud-vue needs special handling.
Running lint after making changes is general good practice. Naive Claude might run npm run lint if asked or as a natural next step after code changes.

notes: Only lint running is general knowledge. The specific webpack mutation patterns (plugins.push(), DefinePlugin preservation, @conduction/nextcloud-vue conditional alias) are all Conduction-specific conventions.
